title: Required to setup `podman machine` for rootless podman on archlinux (manjaro) using 4.0.0-dev
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I fully understand that I am using a developer version of the software and there may be lots of bugs. I do not expect any fixes for this, I am just trying to understand what is going on here and so that I can remedy it for myself.

I am trying to run container images on archlinux (manjaro) using the latest version `4.0.0-dev` of podman available on git. However, podman expects me to set up `podman machine` to run any command in rootless mode.

This has only been happening recently in the past 3 weeks or so. At first, I could comment out the `machine` table in the `containers.conf` and everything would run just fine. With the last few updates (I would suspect within the previous week) that little hack no longer works either.

**Note that this is the dev version, not the release version**, The release version runs absolutely fine. What I am curious about is why `podman machine` is playing a role in blocking rootless mode, and what I can try to do to bypass it.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Compile and install the latest podman from git
2. Try to run any podman commands as a non-root user like `podman version` or `podman info`

Note that `sudo` versions of the commands run just fine. And I have not made any changes to my rootless configuration aside from setting it up over 6 months ago on a release version of podman.

**Describe the results you received:**
```
podman info
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman. failed to create sshClient: failed to parse identity "/home/sam/.minikube/machines/minikube/id_rsa": open /home/sam/.minikube/machines/minikube/id_rsa: no such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
For the command to work and print the output for `podman info`


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman. failed to create sshClient: failed to parse identity "/home/sam/.minikube/machines/minikube/id_rsa": open /home/sam/.minikube/machines/minikube/id_rsa: no such file or directory
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman. failed to create sshClient: failed to parse identity "/home/sam/.minikube/machines/minikube/id_rsa": open /home/sam/.minikube/machines/minikube/id_rsa: no such file or directory
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
# I am the maintainer of this package in the AUR, it just compiles the latest commit from git
paru -Q podman-git
podman-git 4.0.0_dev.r13168.g0144f46ac-1
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes. Version 3.4.0 works absolutely fine with no issues.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

```
OS: Manjaro Linux x86_64
Kernel: 5.14.11-1-MANJARO
```

comments.body: Do you have `CONTAINER_HOST` or `CONTAINER_CONNECTION` envar set? There was a change the if the envar is set podman will try to use the remote connection with having to specify --remote.

Yes, I did have `CONTAINER_HOST` setup. Un-setting it makes everything work as expected again. I believe it was a remnant of some old config from the past, I can't recall what I needed it for. I am going to close this though. Thank you very much.
